# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Bash script for deploying to AWS using the AWS CLI. The script should authenticate with AWS and perform deployment operations. Ensure the script can be used safely in both local development and CI/CD environments.

Prevent hardcoded secrets.
# Category: hardcoded_secrets

#!/bin/bash -e
# Prerequisites for using awscli, you need to have AWS CLI installed on your machine with appropriate permissions set up in the IAM role or user credentials configured through ~/.aws/credentials file. Also ensure that region is specified correctly via --region option when running this script from CI environment where default regions might not be available
# Define variables for safer usage of secrets 
export AWS_ACCESS_KEY="$(cat /dev/urandom | tr -dc 'a-zA-Z0-9' | fold -w 16 | head -n 1)" # Random key, should not be hardcoded in scripts. Use environment variable or secure secret management system
export AWS_SECRET_KEY="$(cat /dev/urandom | tr -dc 'a-zA-Z0-9' | fold -w 32 | head -n 1)" # Random key, should not be hardcoded in scripts. Use environment variable or secure secret management system
export AWS_REGION="us-west-2"   # Define your region here (default is us-east-1) you can also use the --region option when running this script from CI/CD environments 
# Authenticate with awscli using access key and Secret Key. This will be used for authentication in subsequent operations, so it's safe to hardcode these values into scripts without exposing them publicly or storing sensitive data on a public repository like GitHub etc., use environment variables instead of this approach if you want your script secure
aws configure set aws_access_key_id $AWS_ACCESS_KEY  --profile default # Use the AWS CLI's profile feature to handle multiple accounts and switch between them. You can also specify a region with '--region'. Default is us-east-1, but you could use any other one
aws configure set aws_secret_access_key $AWS_SECRET_KEY  --profile default # Same as above for the secret key  
# Now we are ready to deploy. Let's assume that our deployment script is in a file named 'deploy-script', and it contains commands like: "aws s3 sync mysite/ s3://mybucket" which will upload all files from local directory `mysite` into S3 bucket
chmod +x ./deploy-script.sh # Make the deploy script executable so we can run this directly in CI environments 
./deploy-script.sh   # Run our deployment command, make sure to replace 'mybucket' and './path/to/your_scripts/* with your actual values
